summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that parsing a deeply nested XML document (15000 levels) with xmldom DOMParser triggers an unbounded recursive normalize() call, producing a RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded crash.
expected_security_impact: Parsing a deeply nested XML document should cause the DOMParser to crash with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded during the automatic normalize() step at the end of parsing, demonstrating denial of service.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, mounted the vulnerable xmldom repository, and executed the exploit script. The script generated a 15000-level nested XML document and called DOMParser.parseFromString(). The process crashed with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded inside doc.normalize(), confirming the stack overflow vulnerability.
verification_method: The exploit script runs inside the container with Node.js, generates deeply nested XML, calls parseFromString, and checks that a RangeError with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' message is thrown. It writes a proof file only when this exact error is caught, distinguishing the vulnerability from unrelated crashes or parse errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit script caught a RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded thrown from the normalize() call path during parseFromString.
The proof file /tmp/evohunt-proof was written only after confirming the error message matches the expected stack overflow pattern.
The SAX parsing phase completed successfully (the iterative parser handles deep nesting), but the mandatory post-parse normalize() step overflowed the call stack.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
